summary: Follow-up to MPI-189: revert Pod LTX transformer GGUF->bf16. cu130 killed the ~5min cold tax that was GGUFs only reason to exist (MPI-168). bf16 = better quality + kills the MPI-185 dequant OOM + unwinds the engine-split. GATED on MPI-189 shipping + a cu130 Pod confirming bf16 faults ~11s and fits 32GB through both stages. Closes MPI-185 when confirmed.

todo->doing. MPI-189 shipped cu130 (live-proven both arches). Executing bf16 revert CODE now (user-chosen: code revert now, KEEP GGUF files/deps on disk; clean full revert, no VRAM tier). engines block collapses to bf16-both -> delete block, move ltx23-transformer-bf16 into dependencies. GGUF deletion + MPI-185 close GATED on user live cold-bf16-on-cu130 confirm (~11s + 32GB fit) — that proof rolls to this card per MPI-189 event 10 (never had a warm/cold bf16-cu130 baseline).

CODE REVERT DONE (agent-side, reversible). 3 files: (1) models.js — deleted engines: block, moved ltx23-transformer-bf16 into dependencies (both engines bf16, same workflows, no _gguf suffix); (2) generate_ltx.py — 8->4 workflows (dropped GGUF flavour loop + deleted dead loader-swap machinery: LOADER_PAIRS/_select_loader/_select_loaders/_iter_input_refs; user removes the parked UnetLoaderGGUF from the ComfyUI template); (3) resolve-model-deps.test.cjs — real-LTX assertions flipped bf16-both, ALL 12 PASS. NOT deleted (gated on user live confirm): 4x _gguf workflow files + ltx23-transformer-gguf/ComfyUI-GGUF deps + yamlHelper unet_gguf map. DECISIVE LIVE TEST = 4090 (24GB), NOT 5090: 4090 is where GGUF OOMs TODAY (MPI-185 dequant upcast spike); bf16 has no dequant -> win-condition is bf16 i2v runs CLEAN on 4090 no-OOM. 5090 GGUF baseline recorded: cold 3min, warm 25s, medium tier, 1s i2v. User will: remove GGUF loader+quant node from template, then re-gen 4 workflows, then live-test 4090 bf16.

LIVE-PROVEN on 4090 24GB cu130 Pod (bf16). i2v 2-stage COMPLETED, NO OOM (VRAM parked 22-23/24, offload to RAM) — the exact card where GGUF OOMs today via dequant. MPI-185 confirmed dead (no dequant = no spike). Cold first-run 5m26s, warm i2v 1m54s. Deleted 4 orphaned _gguf workflow files (git rm; zero code resolves _gguf filenames now). DEFERRED (NOT deleted): ltx23-transformer-gguf + ComfyUI-GGUF DEP entries — ComfyUI-GGUF (city96) is a HARD import-dep of KJNodes GGUFLoaderKJ (handoff 60c42614), so removing it + node_lock.json + yamlHelper unet_gguf map needs a Pod-side KJNodes-load check first; they are inert (no model resolves them) so safe to leave. maturity=validating. REMAINING: user removes GGUF loader+quant node from ComfyUI template (their step) + the deferred dep-entry cleanup as a separate low-risk task. PERF GAP FOUND + carded MPI-191: 60-93s stage1->stage2 re-fault (aimdo evicts the 40GB between subgraphs; ~92% of continue is reload, ~5s real). Root-caused in pod-perf-investigation.md, pre-existing, bf16&GGUF identical — NOT a revert issue. Sage confirmed NOT the gap (SDPA both sides, gated off on Ada sm_89 local+Pod).

validating -> done (accepted). FULLY CLOSED. Deferred GGUF cleanup finished: deleted ltx23-transformer-gguf + ComfyUI-GGUF DEP entries (dependencies.js) + yamlHelper unet_gguf folder-map + resolveModelDeps _gguf doc examples + node_lock.json GGUF entry (BOTH copies: mpi-ci + dev_configs). Q8 weights deleted from R2 (user). KJNodes HARD-IMPORT FEAR DISPROVEN: cloned pinned KJNodes 7f43f2c — GGUFLoaderKJ imports city96 LAZILY (inside the load method, guarded, friendly-errors if absent), NOT at module load; __init__ imports only the class object. So KJNodes loads clean with NO ComfyUI-GGUF present; our LTX uses UNETLoader(bf16), never touches GGUFLoaderKJ. IMAGE was NEVER affected: ComfyUI-GGUF had NO installOnEngine flag -> Dockerfile bakes only installOnEngine:true nodes -> GGUF was never baked (it was a model dep, correctly URL-lock-only). Nothing to remove from image build. Tests 12/12. 4-file LTX gen proven live (no gguf). template verified GGUF-node-free (single UNETLoader).
